paper.$description: Page surface, light mode. Slate-gray, not warm cream — chosen to sit in conversation with the violet→cyan brand gradient rather than fight it.

paper-dark.$description: Page surface, dark mode. Anchored on ink so brand violet keeps its presence against it.

paper-static.$description: Non-themed paper — for surfaces intentionally light regardless of theme.

ink-dark.$description: Body text, dark mode. Chosen for WCAG AA headroom: text-ink/50 hits ~5.0:1 vs. an earlier #E5EAF0 candidate at ~4.4:1 (AA-borderline).

ink-static.$description: Non-themed ink — for surfaces intentionally dark regardless of theme (e.g. footer, LocaleSwitcher popover).

surface.$description: Elevated card color, light mode. Replaces literal bg-white on neutral cards (not on gradient overlays or buttons that must stay literal white).

surface-dark.$description: Elevated card color, dark mode — lighter than paper so cards read as raised.

violet.$description: Primary brand color. Left anchor of the hero/nav/footer gradient.

sky.$description: Secondary brand color. Right anchor of the hero/nav/footer gradient.

violet-deep.$description: Deeper violet anchor used only in the hero gradient for dimension.

red.$description: Pan-African flag (Marcus Garvey/UNIA, 1920) red — not the African Union's red/gold/green, which an earlier pass used by mistake. Homepage hero only: 'Africa's' letter-group split and its hover-swap silhouette gradient.

hairline-ink-dark.$description: Dark-mode row-divider alpha — bumped from the light-mode 0.10 equivalent so dividers keep presence on dark surfaces.

font-display.$description: Headline face. Chosen for a research/scientific posture — quiet authority over editorial drama (replaced Playfair Display, which read as fashion-mag).

font-mono.$description: Base mono face for data labels.

font-accent-african.$description: African-designed accent face (OFL, Taurai Valerie Mtake). Used for program wordmarks only (Dynamerge hero) — ships one weight (400), never body text or nav.

font-accent-african-secondary.$description: African-designed secondary accent (OFL, Chisaokwu Joboson). Reads too heavy at hero scale (tried and pulled as wordmark face) — kept for short labels like eyebrows and stat callouts at wght=500.

font-accent-african-tertiary.$description: African-designed accent face (OFL, Seun Badejo). Single-word wordmark-scale accents only — currently the homepage hero's lead word ('Africa's'). Instanced at one static weight despite the source shipping a variable MORF axis; don't reach for it outside a standalone word at display size. Replaced Matemasie in this role after a live side-by-side of 8 African OFL faces.

font-mono-african.$description: In-house African-designed monospace — a fontTools re-metricing of Tac One (Afrotype, OFL) onto a fixed-width grid with hand-fixed disambiguation glyphs (serifed capital I, dotted zero). Renamed per OFL §3 as a Modified Version. See docs/TYPOGRAPHY-SYSTEM.md.

brand.$description: Used for the .bg-gradient-brand utility, hairline stripes, gradient borders, and gradient text.

hero.$description: Full-bleed hero background — deliberate brand-mark territory, not an editorial surface. Do not propose replacing this with a flat/neutral hero without being asked.
